meta.name: BBQ Baked Beans (Sweet-Smoky, Stovetop or Oven)

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Classic BBQ baked beans designed for cookouts and meal prep.
Works with canned beans for speed or dry beans if you want from-scratch control.

yield.notes: About 8 side servings (1/2 cup) or 12 smaller servings.

ingredients.label: Beans (canned), drained and rinsed
ingredients.notes: Use navy beans, pinto beans, or great northern. (3 x 15–16 oz cans).

ingredients.label: BBQ sauce
ingredients.notes: Tomato-based BBQ sauce works best. Adjust sweetness/heat to taste.

ingredients.label: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Start with 1/4 cup; increase if your BBQ sauce is not sweet.

ingredients.label: Molasses
ingredients.notes: Optional but very classic for depth.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: Balances sweetness. Use white vinegar if needed.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust at end—BBQ sauce and ketchup can be salty.

ingredients.label: Bacon, diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional—renders fat and adds smoky flavor. Substitute smoked sausage pieces if preferred.

ingredients.label: Smoked sausage, sliced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional—kielbasa/hot link adds body; great when serving as a main-ish side.

steps.title: Prep aromatics and optional meat
steps.body: Dice onion (and pepper if using). Mince garlic. If using bacon, dice; if using smoked sausage, slice into coins/half-moons.

steps.title: Optional: render bacon and sauté aromatics
steps.body: If using bacon: cook in a Dutch oven or deep skillet over medium heat until fat renders and bacon starts to crisp (about 6–8 minutes). Add onion (and bell pepper) and sauté until softened (3–5 minutes). Add garlic for 30 seconds until fragrant. If not using bacon, sauté onion in 1 tbsp oil instead.

steps.title: Build the BBQ bean sauce
steps.body: Stir in BBQ sauce, ketchup (optional), mustard (optional), brown sugar, molasses (optional), vinegar (optional), Worcestershire (optional), smoked paprika/chili powder/pepper. Bring to a gentle simmer. Taste: it should be a little sweeter and slightly tangy—beans will mellow it.

steps.title: Add beans (and optional sausage) and simmer
steps.body: Stir in drained/rinsed beans. If using smoked sausage, add it now. Keep at a low simmer for 10 minutes to marry flavors. If too thick, add a splash of water; if too thin, simmer uncovered.

steps.title: Bake (recommended) or finish on stovetop
steps.body: Oven method (best flavor): transfer to a baking dish or keep in oven-safe Dutch oven and bake at 325°F for 40–50 minutes, stirring once halfway, until thick and glossy. Stovetop method: simmer gently, uncovered, 20–30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Avoid high heat to prevent scorching.

steps.title: Adjust seasoning and serve
steps.body: Taste and adjust: add more vinegar for tang, more sugar for sweetness, more salt/pepper as needed. Serve hot alongside brisket, ribs, pulled pork, BBQ chicken, or sausages.

refrigerate.instructions: Cool quickly, then refrigerate in a sealed container.

freeze.instructions: Freeze in flat bags or containers for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in fridge.

reheat.instructions: Reheat gently on stovetop with a splash of water, or microwave covered, stirring halfway.
reheat.notes: Avoid high heat; sugars can scorch.

leftovers.notes: These thicken as they cool—add a splash of water when reheating.

serveModes.label: Hearty Beans
serveModes.notes: Add smoked sausage or chopped brisket for a heartier bowl.

serveModes.label: Topping
serveModes.notes: Spoon on hot dogs/sausages or over baked potatoes.

notes: Slow cooker: after building sauce and adding beans, cook on LOW 3–4 hours or HIGH 1.5–2 hours; vent lid near end if too thin.
Smoker: place beans in a foil pan and smoke at 250–275°F for 1.5–2 hours, stirring once, for extra smoke flavor.
More tang (Carolina-ish): increase vinegar to 2–3 tbsp and reduce brown sugar slightly.
More sweet (Kansas City-ish): increase brown sugar to 1/3 cup and use a sweeter BBQ sauce.
More heat: add jalapeño or a pinch of cayenne; finish with hot sauce.
Vegetarian: omit bacon/sausage and use a vegetarian BBQ sauce/Worcestershire alternative.
Lower sugar: reduce brown sugar by half and choose a lower-sugar BBQ sauce; flavor with smoked paprika + vinegar.
